question_text: Two springs are connected in series (one after the other). Spring A has a spring constant of 400 N/m and Spring B has a spring constant of 200 N/m. When a 100 N force is applied to the combination, what is the total extension?
explanation: In series, the same force acts on each spring. Extension of A: e₁ = 100/400 = 0.25 m. Extension of B: e₂ = 100/200 = 0.5 m. Total extension = 0.25 + 0.5 = 0.75 m. Note: The softer spring stretches more under the same force.
